rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 1, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD ASSIGNEE LANDMARK COLLECTION SERVICES INC. Granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. Counsel for Landmark must include language in the proposed order on the Judicial Council form to the effect that, unless an attorney appears for it, Landmark may not take any action in court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with the above and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PURPLE STAR MD COLLECTIVE, A CALIFORNIA VS. JACKSAM CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION, DOING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 2, CROSS-DEFENDANT PROCEPACK, INC's Motion for Leave to File a Cross-Complaint. Cross-defendant Procepack, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. Procepack may file its proposed cross-complaint no later than April 27, 2026. Whether as a compulsory or permissive cross-complaint, Procepack has shown good cause for leave to file its proposed cross-complaint and the filing of that cross-complaint will not cause any legal prejudice to defendant Purple Star MD Collective. Nor has Purple Star shown that Procepack acted in bad faith. While Procepack should have sought leave to file its cross-complaint shortly after the court vacated the order to show cause re dismissal on October 24, 2025, Procepack's delay is not significant since all parties knew that both Purple Star and Procepack would be asserting claims and defenses relating to their February 2024 settlement at the re-set trial date of July 20, 2026. (See, e.g., Procepack's response to the OSC filed October 21, 2025 which precipitated the vacating of the osc and the case management statements filed by Purple Star on November 14, 2025 and by Procepack on November 26, 2025). Purple Star's opposition to this motion on the grounds that the cross-complaint radically expands this case from a simple collection action contradicts the parties' filings and does not withstand scrutiny. Since at least March 18, 2025 Purple Star knew that, if there was to be a trial after the parties' settlement agreement, that trial would inevitably need to deal with the "elephant in the room" why their settlement agreement had not extinguished this case. (See Purple Star's response to the osc filed March 18, 2025). In sum, once the osc was vacated, this case was necessarily about the parties' respective positions regarding the settlement agreement and it is only a matter of time till the parties' pleadings catch up to that reality. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: A2 ACADEMY LLC ET AL VS. SWORD HEALTH, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 10, PLAINTIFFS A2 ACADEMY LLC, KATHRYN THOMAS, STEPHEN JOHNSTON, and ARNOLD WHITMAN's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is granted. Plaintiffs may file their "updated" proposed third amended complaint no later than April 27, 2026. With the deletion of paragraph 58 in the earlier version of the proposed third amended complaint, the updated proposed third amended complaint is no longer inconsistent with the order filed July 10, 2025. Defendants have not shown either prejudice to them if plaintiffs were granted leave to file a third amended complaint or excessive delay by plaintiffs in seeking to file a third amended complaint. Nor have defendants shown that the third amended complaint is a sham pleading. All of defendants' arguments directed to the sufficiency of the third amended complaint are more properly raised in a pleading motion such as a demurrer or a motion to strike rather than in opposition to a motion to amend. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: KRISTINA VEACO VS. GLOBAL MEDICAL RESPONSE,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendants American Medical Response West And Tegan Durham Reiss To Plaintiff?S Request For Production Of Documents Set 1, Number 18, And Set 2, Numbers 19-53
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KRISTINA VEACO's Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendants American Medical Response West And Tegan Durham Reiss To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents Set 1, Number 18, And Set 2, Numbers 19-53. Plaintiff Kristina Veaco's motion to compel further responses from defendants American Medical Response West and Tegan Reiss to Veaco's set one and set two document requests is granted. No later than May 13, 2026 defendants shall produce all responsive documents to set one request 18 and set two requests 19-53 and serve verified code-compliant further responses stating that they have produced all responsive documents. The safety handbook and related training materials and the dispatch logs all meet the standard for discovery relevance. Defendants have not shown that any of the documents should be covered by a confidentiality protective order. The sole evidence that defendants offer in support of their argument for a protective order is the declaration of their attorney. That declaration is necessarily based on hearsay, lacks personal knowledge, and has no probative value on the issue of defendants' need to protect the responsive documents. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. DOPPIO ZERO SF, LP
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 7, 2- PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Alexander Xue's motion to strike portions of defendant Doppio Zero SF, LLP's answer to the complaint is granted as to the first two sentences in the second paragraph on page 1 of the answer, off calendar as to the first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses, and denied as to all other affirmative defenses. Because no notice was given ending the tolling period for filing the motion, the motion is not untimely filed. CCP 431.40 is inapplicable because the defendant did not file a response to the complaint in a "form prescribed by the Judicial Council." The first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses have been dealt with in the ruling on plaintiff's demurrer to the answer. All the other affirmative defenses are sufficiently alleged and are not uncertain per the norms of pleading affirmative defenses in this and other counties. If the plaintiff wishes any further information about the affirmative defenses for which his motion to strike is denied, he may serve appropriate discovery. If the plaintiff believes that there is no merit to any of those affirmative defenses, he may file a motion for summary adjudication. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. DOPPIO ZERO SF, LP
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 7, 1- PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Alexander Xue's demurrer to defendant Doppio Zero SF, LLP's answer to the complaint is sustained as to the first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses without leave to amend and overruled as to all other affirmative defenses. Because no notice was given ending the tolling period for filing the demurrer, the demurrer is not untimely filed. CCP 431.40 is inapplicable because the defendant did not file a response to the complaint in a "form prescribed by the Judicial Council." The first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses are not true affirmative defenses. All the other affirmative defenses are sufficiently alleged and are not uncertain per the norms of pleading affirmative defenses in this and other counties. If the plaintiff wishes any further information about the affirmative defenses for which his demurrer is overruled, he may serve appropriate discovery. If the plaintiff believes that there is no merit to any of those affirmative defenses, he may file a motion for summary adjudication. rulings.Case Title: X CORP. VS. ERIK MCAFEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Seal Materials Exhibits To Proposed Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 8, PLAINTIFF X CORP.'s Motion To Seal Materials Exhibits To Proposed Amended Cross-Complaint. Plaintiff X Corp.'s motion to seal exhibits to the stipulation for the filing of an amended cross-complaint is denied without prejudice to re-filing the motion based on evidentiary facts, not conclusions, supporting each of the required findings of CRC 2.550(d). That the exhibits sought to be sealed were produced per the confidentiality terms of a protective order and the defendant does not object to the sealing request are insufficient to support a sealing order. (CRC 2.551(a)). A sealing order may only be granted based on a declaration "containing facts" supporting a finding of each of the five requirements of CRC 2.550(d). (CRC 2.551(b)). The declaration of Zaur Gajiev falls far short of meeting this standard. The material portions of the declaration are largely conclusory and do not identify by page, paragraph, specific information or any other way the information claimed to require a sealing order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: PAUL REEB VS. AIR EDUCATION, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Line 9, PLAINTIFF PAUL REEB's Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Special Interrogatories. Plaintiff Paul Reeb's motion to compel further responses to his first set of special interrogatories is denied as to interrogatories 1-3, granted as to interrogatories 10 and 12, and granted in part as to interrogatory 13. Reeb's requests for information about non-California tutors (interrogatories 1 and 2) and tutoring sessions conducted by non-California tutors (interrogatory 3) are premature. Unless and until there is a ruling that the pertinent California Labor Code provisions apply to California tutors, providing this information imposes an unwarranted burden on defendant Air Education, Inc. Reeb's requests for information about schools and school districts who have been customers or clients of Air Education (interrogatories 10 and 12) are discovery relevant and all of Air Education's objections to providing that information lack merit. No later than May 13, 2026 Air Education must serve verified code-compliant further responses to interrogatories 10 and 12 providing all responsive information. Reed's request for contact information for officers of Air Education (interrogatory 13) is permissible as to those persons who are no longer employed by and are not agents of Air Education, but not as to those persons who are currently employed by or are agents of Air Education, as to whom it can contact through Air Education's attorneys. No later than May 13, 2026 Air Education must serve verified code-compliant further responses to interrogatory 13 providing all responsive information as to those persons who are not currently employed by and are not currently agents of Air Education. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
